text: Activities are frequently highlighted as an important pedagogical resource in the science teaching classroom due to their pedagogical functions in arousing interest for nature and developing critical thinking and the students’ ecological perception of the environment. However, teaching activities are still primarily based on lecturing. This paper aims to reflect on the activities in the practical classes and characterize the two public schools in Vitória de Santo Antão, PE—from the Duque de Caxias school (in the rural area) and the August 3 school (in the urban area). We conducted the study in the classrooms of these schools, by means of visitations and by applying questionnaires to science teachers from the 6th to 9th grades of these schools. The results obtained indicate that Duque de Caxias school, when compared with the August 3 school, is characterized by being in a better condition as far as infrastructure is concerned, as well as by the educators’ involvement in the practical classes. In both institutions, the main difficulties encountered for the realization of the experimental activities carried out in the classroom were lack of materials, crowded classrooms and lack of time. As a way of contributing to improving the realization of these activities, this paper presents workbooks that are educational tools, elaborated for students from the 6th to 9th grades, aligned to the Brazilian curriculum. The objective is to encourage, both for the teacher and for the student, the access to practical activities that make the student familiarize himself with the practical aspects of the science taught in the classroom.
